title: Clean Energy Breaks Ground on Three Renewable Natural Gas Dairy Projects With Maas Energy Works
content:
Clean Energy Fuels Corp. has begun construction on three renewable natural gas (RNG) production facilities under its development agreement with Maas Energy Works. These projects, spanning six dairies across South Dakota, Georgia, Florida, and New Mexico, will capture methane from a combined herd of about 24,300 dairy cows and are expected to produce roughly three million gallons of RNG annually. The fuel will power heavy-duty trucks, transit, and vocational fleets nationwide through Clean Energyâ€™s 600+ fueling stations, providing a steady supply of negative-carbon fuel.

Maas Energy Works employs a cost-effective covered lagoon system rather than traditional anaerobic digester tanks, reducing costs and improving efficiency in capturing methane from manure. Together, the companies are investing approximately $80 million, with completion targeted for 2026.

RNG from dairy waste significantly cuts greenhouse gas emissions compared with diesel, contributing to U.S. climate goals. Agriculture and transportation together account for nearly 38% of U.S. GHG emissions, making the capture and reuse of methane an important mitigation strategy. Clean Energy also retains the option to develop additional projects with Maas Energy Works in the future as market conditions evolve, underscoring its commitment to scaling renewable, low-carbon fuels nationwide.
author: Analysts Account
